concentration.headline: Strong urban primacy — capital drives the country
concentration.body: Ho Chi Minh City dominates Vietnam's urban hierarchy with 27.7% of tracked urban population across 180 cities. The gap between Ho Chi Minh City and the second city is substantial, reflecting a historically centralised national structure.
concentration.comparison: Similar to: France, Mexico, South Korea

gravity.headline: Dense gravitational cluster — Ho Chi Minh City & Thủ Đức
gravity.body: The strongest gravitational pull exists between Ho Chi Minh City and Thủ Đức (9 km apart, combined population 16,149,795). Cities form a tight gravitational cluster with top pairs averaging 16 km apart. This dense sub-regional network often functions as a single megalopolitan economy.
gravity.top_pair: Ho Chi Minh City → Thủ Đức

narration.synthesis: Vietnam shows clear urban primacy: Ho Chi Minh City accounts for 27.7% of tracked population. The gravity network (Ho Chi Minh City → Thủ Đức, avg 16 km) reinforces this — economic mass concentrates in and around the dominant city, with secondary cities playing supporting rather than competing roles.
